records.ety: [OE. fugitif, F. fugitif, fr. L. fugitivus, fr. fugere to flee. See Bow to bend, and cf. Feverfew.]

defs: Fleeing from pursuit, danger, restraint, etc., escaping, from service, duty etc.; as, a fugitive solder; a fugitive slave; a fugitive debtor.
quotes: Can a fugitive daughter enjoy herself while her parents are in tear?
A libellous pamphlet of a fugitive physician.

defs: Not fixed; not durable; liable to disappear or fall away; volatile; uncertain; evanescent; liable to fade; -- applied to material and immaterial things; as, fugitive colors; a fugitive idea.
quotes: The me more tender and fugitive parts, the leaves . . . of vegatables.

defs: One who flees from pursuit, danger, restraint, service, duty, etc.; a deserter; as, a fugitive from justice.
Something hard to be caught or detained.
quotes: Or Catch that airy fugitive called wit.
